title: galium helveticum
intro: Galium helveticum, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb native to Europe and North Africa, characterized by its small white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of ascorbic acid, which makes it an effective treatment for scurvy and other vitamin C deficiencies. The health benefits of Galium helveticum include its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related disorders. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
